name: Steve Allen
description: American actor, comedian, musician and presenter (1921–2000)
abstract: Stephen Valentine Patrick William Allen (December 26, 1921 – October 30, 2000) was an American television and radio personality, comedian, musician, composer, writer, and actor. Though he got his start in radio, he is best known for his extensive network television career.

interviews.title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ellen Rowe
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Steve Allen — I was lucky to interview Steve Allen years ago.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellen Rowe interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 2, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Yes. Steve Allen inherited me. We had a show at NBC back in I guess about 1965... And we’ve had a very pleasant relationship... I conducted three different shows that he had out here... He’s fun to play with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: we became very good friends. I brought you some pictures of he and I together... I’m indebted to him quite a lot because he’s done a lot for me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Steve Allen was my friend. I was friends with Steve in the early 40s, late 40s. He became my friend then he became a big fan of mine
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: my repartee with Steve Allen was about as good as could be expected and I enjoyed being with him, working with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Rosengarden
about_quotes.evidence: And then, in those days the guy came along, Steve Allen, and we’re still good friends.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Rosengarden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 6, 1996
